text: The first law of thermodynamics is:
options: Heat flows from hot to cold
explanation: First Law: \( \Delta U = Q - W \). Internal energy change = heat absorbed − work done by system.
name: First Law of Thermodynamics

explanation: Adiabatic: thermally insulated system, \( Q = 0 \). Work done by gas comes from internal energy: \( W = -\Delta U \).
name: Adiabatic Process

text: For an ideal gas, the adiabatic process follows:
explanation: Adiabatic process: \( PV^\gamma = \text{const} \), where \( \gamma = C_p/C_v > 1 \). More steep than isothermal curve on P-V diagram.
name: Adiabatic Process Equation

text: The efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between temperatures \(T_H\) and \(T_C\) is:
explanation: Carnot efficiency: \( \eta = 1 - \frac{T_C}{T_H} = \frac{T_H - T_C}{T_H} \). This is the maximum possible efficiency for any heat engine.
name: Carnot Efficiency

text: The second law of thermodynamics implies:
options: Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot body (Clausius statement)
explanation: 2nd Law (Clausius): heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot. (Kelvin-Planck): impossible to convert all heat to work.
name: Second Law of Thermodynamics

text: In an isothermal process for an ideal gas, work done is:
explanation: Isothermal work: \( W = nRT\ln\frac{V_2}{V_1} = nRT\ln\frac{P_1}{P_2} \). Temperature is constant → \( \Delta U = 0 \Rightarrow Q = W \).
name: Isothermal Work

text: The coefficient of performance (COP) of a refrigerator is:
explanation: COP of refrigerator: \( COP = Q_L/W = T_C/(T_H - T_C) \) for Carnot refrigerator. Higher COP = more efficient cooling.
name: COP of Refrigerator

text: For an ideal gas, \( C_p - C_v = \)
explanation: Meyer's relation: \( C_p - C_v = R \) for one mole of ideal gas. \(C_p > C_v\) because extra work done in constant pressure expansion.
name: Meyer's Relation

text: The entropy change \(\Delta S\) for a reversible process at temperature \(T\) is:
explanation: Entropy change: \( dS = dQ_{rev}/T \), or \( \Delta S = Q/T \) for isothermal reversible process. For reversible adiabatic: \( \Delta S = 0 \).
name: Entropy Change

text: In an isochoric (constant volume) process, work done is:
explanation: Isochoric: \( \Delta V = 0 \Rightarrow W = 0 \). All heat supplied goes to internal energy: \( Q = \Delta U = nC_v\Delta T \).
name: Isochoric Process Work

text: For a monatomic ideal gas, \(\gamma = C_p/C_v = \)
explanation: Monatomic (He, Ar): 3 degrees of freedom. \( C_v = \frac{3}{2}R \), \( C_p = \frac{5}{2}R \), \( \gamma = 5/3 \approx 1.67 \).
name: γ for Monatomic Gas

text: A heat engine absorbs \(Q_H = 1000\,\text{J}\) and rejects \(Q_C = 600\,\text{J}\). Its efficiency is:
name: Heat Engine Efficiency Calculation

options: Pressure and temperature along a phase boundary
explanation: Clausius-Clapeyron: \( \frac{dP}{dT} = \frac{L}{T\Delta V} \). Describes how phase transition pressure varies with temperature.
name: Clausius-Clapeyron Equation

text: In a cyclic process, the net change in internal energy is:
options: Zero (state function returns to initial state)
explanation: Internal energy is a state function. In a complete cycle, system returns to initial state: \( \Delta U_{cycle} = 0 \Rightarrow Q_{net} = W_{net} \).
name: Cyclic Process Internal Energy

text: Which process is represented by a vertical line on a P-V diagram?
explanation: Isochoric (constant volume): vertical line on P-V diagram. Isobaric (constant pressure): horizontal line. Isothermal: hyperbola (PV=const).
name: P-V Diagram Processes
